description: Consider the following transformation of prime numbers: If b(n) == 1 (mod 4) then b(n+1) = (b(n) + 1)/2. If b(n) == 3 (mod 4) then b(n+1) = (b(n) - 1)/2. Continue this transformation until a nonprime is reached. a(n) is the least prime number such that exactly n primes appear in the chain of numbers created by this transformation.
